quick_facts.label: Qualification
quick_facts.value: B.Sc. (Hons.) Forestry is one of the 13 four-year bachelor’s degree programmes covered by ICAR’s CUET (ICAR-UG) 2026 admission bulletin; it is listed as programme code 04.

quick_facts.label: Typical entry subjects
quick_facts.value: For the CUET (ICAR-UG) 2026 route, candidates must have passed Class 12 with at least three core subjects from Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology and Agriculture/Inter-Agriculture. Individual universities can impose additional subject combinations, age limits or other conditions.

quick_facts.label: Academic focus
quick_facts.value: ICAR’s minimum standards identify core forestry areas including silviculture and agroforestry, forest biology and tree improvement, natural-resource management, forest-products utilization, wildlife sciences, and basic and social sciences.

quick_facts.label: National forest-service pathway
quick_facts.value: A bachelor’s degree in Forestry is expressly accepted as an educational qualification for the UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination, subject to the full annual examination rules.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: For admission through the ICAR quota in the 2026–27 cycle, applicants needed a recognized 10+2/equivalent qualification and at least three Class 12 core subjects drawn from Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology and Agriculture/Inter-Agriculture. They also had to take CUET subjects corresponding to those passed at Class 12; taking different subjects could lead to rejection during counselling or admission.

ICAR’s published minimum standards for establishing a B.Sc. (Hons.) Forestry college describe the expected entry background as Intermediate/10+2 with PCM, PCMB or Agriculture. Actual eligibility is not identical across all institutions: the ICAR bulletin lists programme-specific combinations and sometimes university-specific age or other requirements. Applicants should therefore verify the target university’s current prospectus before applying.
bullets: Check that your Class 12 subjects match both the CUET (ICAR-UG) requirements and the specific university/programme entry listed in the current ICAR counselling bulletin.
Treat university-level conditions, including subject combinations and age rules, as controlling for that institution.

sections.heading: Duration and award
paragraphs: In the ICAR CUET (ICAR-UG) 2026 bulletin, B.Sc. (Hons.) Forestry is among the bachelor’s programmes stated to have a four-year duration. The same bulletin identifies undergraduate agricultural-science degrees in this admission framework as professional degrees.
bullets: Confirm the academic calendar, credit rules, internship/fieldwork requirements and exit provisions in the admitting university’s regulations.

sections.heading: What the programme covers
paragraphs: The degree is designed around scientific and applied forest-resource management rather than only tree identification or plantation work. ICAR’s forestry standards organize provision around silviculture and agroforestry; forest biology and tree improvement; natural-resource management; forest-products utilization; wildlife sciences; and basic and social sciences. The precise paper titles, practicals, credits and electives remain university-specific.

A university curriculum can additionally include applied areas such as wood science and technology, forest hydrology and watershed management, forest economics and marketing, forest extension and community forestry, forest law and policy, geomatics, restoration ecology, plantation forestry, and non-timber forest products. These examples illustrate curriculum variation rather than a nationwide compulsory paper list.
bullets: Expect a blend of biological sciences, resource management, field-oriented forestry and social/economic dimensions of forest use.
Compare the semester-wise syllabus and practical/field facilities before choosing a college; ICAR’s accreditation listings are a useful starting point for checking institutions and programmes.

sections.heading: Admission through ICAR
paragraphs: For the 2026–27 ICAR quota process, undergraduate admission is based on merit rank in CUET (ICAR-UG) 2026. B.Sc. (Hons.) Forestry is specifically included in that process, and the bulletin publishes participating universities, ICAR-quota seats, accepted Class 12 subject combinations and any special conditions by programme.

This is a route for ICAR quota counselling, not a guarantee that every Forestry college uses the same selection process. Candidates should check the target university’s own admission notice alongside the current CUET and ICAR counselling notices.
bullets: Use the current CUET (ICAR-UG) information bulletin to select eligible test subjects.
After results, follow ICAR’s counselling instructions and validate the programme-specific requirements for each preferred university.
For quality due diligence, consult ICAR’s current accreditation-programme listings in addition to a university’s own recognition and admission information.

sections.heading: Further study and competitive-examination options
paragraphs: A common academic progression is M.Sc. Forestry. ICAR’s AIEEA (PG) is the national entrance route used for postgraduate agriculture and allied-sciences admissions in the ICAR-AU system; eligibility, participating seats, scholarships and other requirements must be checked in that year’s NTA/ICAR information bulletin. For the 2026–27 cycle, ICAR announced AIEEA (PG) registration from May 8 to June 7, 2026, so that application window is already past as of July 18, 2026.

For public-service careers, B.Sc. (Hons.) Forestry meets the named degree-field requirement for the UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination. The 2026 UPSC notice states that candidates must first take and qualify through the Civil Services Preliminary Examination for progression to the Indian Forest Service Main examination, and all age, nationality, medical and other conditions in the relevant annual notice still apply.
bullets: M.Sc. Forestry / allied postgraduate study: monitor the next ICAR AIEEA (PG) notice and the target university’s programme requirements.
UPSC Indian Forest Service: Forestry is an accepted qualifying degree, but eligibility and examination rules must be checked against the notification for the year of application.
State Forest Service recruitment: review the relevant State Public Service Commission notification, because eligibility, selection stages and vacancies vary by state and recruitment cycle.
